sections.heading: Alert Conditions
sections.body:
You can configure the following alert conditions in the project-level
[Configure Alert Settings](configure-alerts) page to trigger alerts.

[System: CPU (Steal) % is](<System: CPU (Steal) % is>) occurs when the CPU usage exceeds the
guaranteed baseline CPU credit accumulation rate by the specified
threshold. For more information on CPU credit accumulation, refer to
the AWS (Amazon Web Services) documentation for [Burstable Performance Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/burstable-performance-instances.html).

> **Note:** The [System: CPU (Steal) % is](<System: CPU (Steal) % is>) alert is applicable when
> the EC2 instance credit balance is exhausted. Atlas triggers
> this alert only for AWS EC2 instances that support [Burstable
> Performance Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/burstable-performance-instances.html).
> Currently, these are `M10` and `M20` cluster types.

[System: CPU (User) % is](<System: CPU (User) % is>) occurs when the CPU usage of the
MongoDB process, as normalized by the number of CPUs, exceeds the
specified threshold.

sections.heading: Common Triggers
sections.body:
[Unoptimized queries](manual/core/query-optimization) might lead to
`System CPU` alerts. Also, your current cluster tier might not
support the current workload.

sections.heading: Fix the Immediate Problem
sections.body:
Consider adding one or more [indexes](manual/indexes) to improve
query performance.

sections.heading: Implement a Long-Term Solution
sections.body:
Consider upgrading your cluster to a higher tier to
reduce the CPU usage percentage utilized by the current workload.
For more information on upgrading a cluster, see
Modify a Cluster.

sections.heading: Monitor Your Progress
sections.body:
View the **Normalized System CPU** chart to monitor CPU usage
of all processes on the node, scaled to a range of 0-100% by dividing
by the number of CPU cores.

Monitor CPU usage to determine whether to scale to a higher cluster tier.

If you are unable to see the usage that triggered the alert, zoom in
on the Normalized System CPU chart by clicking and dragging
your mouse over the period of interest. With a higher-resolution view
you may be able to identify acute spikes in CPU usage that weren't
visible in the overview.

To learn more, see
[performance metrics](monitor-cluster-metrics).
